chapter_title.en: Chapter 69 – The righteousness with the parents

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (429/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (429/ 8)
text: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ إِنِّي قَدْ وَلَدْتُ بِنْتاً وَ رَبَّيْتُهَا حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغَتْ فَأَلْبَسْتُهَا وَ حَلَّيْتُهَا ثُمَّ جِئْتُ بِهَا إِلَى قَلِيبٍ فَدَفَعْتُهَا فِي جَوْفِهِ وَ كَانَ آخِرُ مَا سَمِعْتُ مِنْهَا وَ هِيَ تَقُولُ يَا أَبَتَاهْ فَمَا كَفَّارَةُ ذَلِكَ
قَالَ أَ لَكَ أُمٌّ حَيَّةٌ قَالَ لَا قَالَ فَلَكَ خَالَةٌ حَيَّةٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَابْرَرْهَا فَإِنَّهَا بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْأُمِّ يُكَفِّرْ عَنْكَ مَا صَنَعْتَ

قَالَ أَبُو خَدِيجَةَ فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَتَى كَانَ هَذَا فَقَالَ كَانَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَ كَانُوا يَقْتُلُونَ الْبَنَاتِ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يُسْبَيْنَ فَيَلِدْنَ فِي قَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ .

en.hubeali: Al Washha, from Ahmad Bin Aiz, from Abu Khadeeja,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A man came over to the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and he said, ‘There was a daughter born unto me, and I nourished her until when she became an adult, so I put some new clothes on her and some ornaments, then went with her to a well and pushed her into its middle (to kill her), and the last of what I heard from her was that she was saying, ‘O father ! So what is the expiation of that?’

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘Is there a mother for you, alive?’ He said, ‘No’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘So for you there is a maternal aunt, alive?’ He said, ‘Yes’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘So be righteous with her, for she is at the status of the mother. It would expiate you from what you did’.

Abu Khadeeja (the narrator) said, ‘So I said to Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘When was this?’ So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘During the pre-Islamic period, and they used to kill the daughters fearing that they would be made captives, so they would give birth among other people’.
en.sarwar: 18. Al-Washsha’ has narrated from Ahmad ibn ‘Ai’z from abu Khadijah from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once a man came to the Holy Prophet and said, ‘I had a daughter and I brought her up until she became an adult. I then dressed her up in beautiful clothes, took her to the well and threw her therein to her death and the last thing I heard from her was; O father! What is the expiation for it?’ The Messenger of Allah asked, ‘Do you have a living mother?’ He said, ‘No, I do not.’ The Messenger of Allah asked, ‘Do you have a living maternal aunt?’ He said, ‘Yes, I do.’ The Messenger of Allah said, ‘Be kind to her; she is like a mother, this will serve as expiation for what you have done.’ Abu Khadijah has said, ‘I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘When was this?’ The Imam said, ‘It was in the time of Jahiliyah (age of darkness). They would kill the girls for fear of being taken captive and giving birth to other people.’”
